summary: Iteration E - Code Quality Fixes Regression Testing. Backend: 22/22 tests pass. Frontend: Most fixes verified. One remaining issue: Career salary chart key fix is incomplete (collisions still occur when roles share same start_date+company). Finance table has a pre-existing key collision not in this PR scope.

ui_bugs.issue: Duplicate key warnings still fired: key={p.date + '-' + (p.company || i)} collides when multiple roles share same start_date AND same company. The fallback `i` is only used when p.company is falsy, so identical date+company combos still produce the same key. Fix: include r.id in salaryProgression mapped objects (add id field to map, use key={p.id}).
ui_bugs.selector: Career > Roles tab > salary progression chart bars

ui_bugs.issue: PRE-EXISTING (not in this PR scope): Duplicate key warning in Finance investments Table because two column definitions have k='current_value' (Value column AND Return column). cols.map((c) => <th key={c.k}> and <td key={c.k}> produce duplicate keys. Fix: use c.label as key, or add a unique 'id' field to each col definition.
ui_bugs.selector: Finance > Investments tab table headers and cells

action_items: Fix Career salary progression chart key: include `id` in salaryProgression mapped objects so key={p.id} instead of date+company compound. The `|| i` fallback only activates when company is falsy — identical date+company combos still collide.
Fix Finance investments Table key collision (pre-existing): Two columns both have k='current_value'. Use c.label as key for cols.map in Table component, or add unique id to each col definition.
critical_code_review_comments: Career.jsx line 69: salaryProgression map does not include `id` field. The key at line 146 `key={p.date + '-' + (p.company || i)}` fails when roles have same start_date + company (test data shows this). Proper fix: .map((r) => ({ id: r.id, date: r.start_date, ctc: r.ctc, title: r.title, company: r.company })) and key={p.id}.
Finance.jsx investments cols config (lines 174-186): Two entries with k='current_value' cause duplicate keys in Table component's cols.map. Use k='return_pct' or add unique identifiers per column.

success_rate.backend: 100% (22/22 passed - is True → == True changes working)
success_rate.frontend: 87% - 7/8 features verified passing, Career salary chart key fix incomplete

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration E tested code quality fixes. Backend 22/22 passing. Two key-prop issues remain: (1) Career salary progression chart key collides when roles share same start_date + company - fix by including r.id in salaryProgression .map(). (2) Finance investments Table has pre-existing duplicate key issue with two columns having k='current_value'. All UniversalInbox flows work. Finance investments Return % column works. Career inline edit works. All useMemo changes work correctly.

career_key_regression.issue: Career salary progression chart still shows duplicate key warnings
career_key_regression.root_cause: The key formula key={p.date + '-' + (p.company || i)} uses array index i as fallback ONLY when p.company is falsy. When p.company is truthy (e.g. 'Test Corp'), i is ignored. If two roles have same start_date and same company, their keys collide.
career_key_regression.reproduction: Have 2+ roles with identical start_date and company values with CTC set
career_key_regression.fix: Change salaryProgression .map() at line 69 to include id: r.id, then use key={p.id} at line 146

finance_key_regression.issue: Finance investments table shows 4 duplicate key warnings
finance_key_regression.root_cause: Investments table column definitions have two entries with k='current_value' (the Value column and the Return column). The Table component uses key={c.k} for both th and td cells.
finance_key_regression.fix: Change Return column k to 'return_pct' or any unique string, OR use c.label as key in Table component's cols.map
